purpose: P7 competitive baseline for PARBAUGHS dashboard taste-scoring (>=7.5/10 required to ship).

approach_summary.primary: WebFetch marketing/product pages with prompt asking for screenshot image URLs and visible UI features.
approach_summary.secondary: curl the returned image URLs directly to save PNG/SVG/WEBP locally.
approach_summary.result: All 5 vendors yielded image URLs from a single WebFetch call. 17 images saved locally (3 Linear, 4 Vercel, 2 Stripe, 4 Datadog, 4 Sentry). Vercel returned both light and dark variants on observability page. Sentry images served via Vercel image-proxy on sentry.io (relative paths).

vendors.name: Linear
vendors.approach_worked: Single WebFetch returned 7 image URLs from the marketing homepage hero / scrolling sections. Linear uses Cloudflare image delivery (cdn-cgi/imagedelivery) so all images are dpr=2 high-res PNG.

captures.shows: Main hero product UI (issue list / sidebar nav)
Timeline/planning view with initiatives and project tracking — calendar-row pattern, status pills, dense rows
Analytics dashboard widget — 'Cycle time by agent' scatter plot, two-color comparison (blue/orange), dark theme, single-card composition

observed_state.palette: near-black surface (#0a0a0a-ish), neutral monochrome ink, single brand accent (purple #5E6AD2) plus categorical accents (blue/orange) on charts
observed_state.typography: Custom sans (Inter Display variant), tight tracking, generous line-height, eyebrow labels often lowercase
observed_state.info_density: Medium-high — long lists of rows but very generous whitespace within rows, no chart-stuffing
observed_state.interaction_affordances: Status pills, priority dots, assignee avatars, due-date pills. Hover-to-edit common. Subtle hairline dividers (#1f1f1f).
observed_state.layout: Single-pane focus on one widget at a time when zoomed; multi-pane sidebar+main+right-panel when in-app
observed_state.distinctive: Restraint. Almost no decoration. Single accent color does heavy lifting. Charts breathe.

vendors.name: Vercel
source_pages_fetched: https://vercel.com (homepage - returned no UI screenshots, only marketing illustrations)
https://vercel.com/products/observability (returned 6 dashboard SVGs in light+dark pairs)
vendors.approach_worked: Homepage was useless — only marketing illustrations. Pivoted to /products/observability which returned proper dashboard SVGs with both light and dark variants.

captures.shows: 5XX error rate anomaly chart on /api/auth route with Vercel Agent investigating status — light theme
Same alert investigation chart in dark theme
Monitoring view showing 429 response codes over 2 weeks with data table breakdown by host+path pairs — dark theme
Query configuration UI with property selection, row filtering, grouping — dark theme

observed_state.palette: Pure black/white maximalism. Background #000 in dark, #fff in light. Single neutral palette of ink + 1-3 categorical accents (blue, red for alert). No brand color in chrome.
observed_state.typography: Geist sans (custom Inter-derived), tight cap-height, big numerals for metric callouts
observed_state.info_density: Medium — focused single-chart-per-card, with explicit captions and timestamps. Lots of whitespace around primary chart.
observed_state.interaction_affordances: Query builder UI is conspicuous (filter pills, group-by chips), alert states have AI-investigation banner
observed_state.layout: Card-based, single primary chart per card, often paired with a data-table breakdown below
observed_state.distinctive: Brand-bold via absence of brand color in chrome. Black-and-white maximalism. Sharp typography. AI-investigation as first-class UI element.

vendors.name: Stripe
vendors.approach_worked: Single WebFetch returned a few SVG dashboard mockups. Stripe's marketing relies heavily on stylized vector SVGs of their dashboard rather than raster screenshots.

captures.shows: Payment analytics dashboard — optimization cost metric callout, multi-series line chart with annotation pills
A/B testing visualization comparing payment method performance

observed_state.palette: Off-white surface, deep ink, signature Stripe purple (#635BFF) accent. Categorical chart palette: purple/teal/pink/yellow soft gradients.
observed_state.typography: Sohne (custom sans), generous letterspacing on labels, big metric numerals with subdued unit suffixes
observed_state.info_density: High but premium — many small chart elements per screen but careful hierarchy keeps it readable. Lots of contextual annotations (deltas, percentages, callouts).
observed_state.interaction_affordances: Filter chips, segmented controls, range-pickers heavily used. Cards have hover state with subtle elevation.
observed_state.layout: Multi-column dashboard grid with mixed-height cards. Tile sizes vary semantically — important metrics get larger tiles.
observed_state.distinctive: Premium-feel density. Single signature brand color tightly controlled. Soft gradient washes in charts. Annotations and contextual deltas everywhere.

vendors.name: Datadog
vendors.approach_worked: Single WebFetch returned 8 dashboard PNG URLs from the static CDN (corp.dd-static.net). 4 most-representative downloaded.

captures.shows: The canonical Datadog dashboard: multi-widget grid with stacked area charts, big number tiles, event list, top-N bar chart, alert overlay — classic ultra-dense layout
Alert configuration interface with metric thresholds
Real User Monitoring dashboard correlating frontend perf with business impact
Cloud Network Monitoring dashboard — service map + dependency graph

observed_state.theme_default: light (despite reputation — current marketing screenshots show light dashboards)
observed_state.palette: White surface with light gray hairlines. Categorical chart palette: 6-8 colors (blue, purple, teal, yellow, orange) for multi-series. Severity: red bands for alerts, green for OK status.
observed_state.typography: Open Sans / system stack, smaller body text than peers (12px-ish), tight rows in dense tables
observed_state.info_density: Ultra-high. 6-10 widgets per dashboard screen. Time-series charts dominate. Multi-series stacked area is common. Event streams with severity icons running down the side.
observed_state.interaction_affordances: Drag-to-resize widget handles visible. Toolbox of widget types at top (Free Text / Graph / Query Value / Toplist / Event Timeline / Event Stream / Image / Note / Alert Graph / Alert Value / IFrame). Annotations, alert overlays in red box on top of charts.
observed_state.layout: Free-form grid. Widget palette at top. Note tiles (yellow sticky-note style) and IFrame embeds first-class.
observed_state.distinctive: Maximum info density. Widget toolbox model — every metric is a configurable tile. Multi-color categorical palette without restraint. Alert overlays as red-bordered boxes that sit over charts.

vendors.name: Sentry
source_pages_fetched: https://sentry.io (returned no images — landing redirects to product docs)
vendors.approach_worked: Root sentry.io returned no images. /welcome/ is the canonical product-tour page. Sentry serves images via Vercel image-proxy under /_vercel/image with url= query param — still fetchable via curl.

captures.shows: Session Replay interface — user session with navigation events and inline error indicators (out-of-stock, 500 server error markers)
Issue detail — unhandled exception in src/main.py with code context, release info, related file changes
Issue detail — fatal read ETIMEDOUT error with root cause analysis and stack trace

observed_state.palette: Deep purple-black surface (#1a1429-ish), Sentry signature purple/pink brand accent. Severity: red/orange for errors (fatal/error), yellow for warning, blue for info. Code snippets get distinct mono-syntax-highlight panel.
observed_state.typography: Sans for chrome (Rubik), mono for code (JetBrains-Mono-ish), mixed sizes for inline contextual data
observed_state.info_density: High in detail views (code + stack trace + breadcrumbs all visible), lower on overview cards
observed_state.interaction_affordances: Severity badges (fatal/error/warning), event count + user count side-by-side, related-files chips, breadcrumb timeline with icons
observed_state.layout: Side-rail navigation + main detail pane + collapsible context panel. Issue detail is the canonical surface — heavy on code context.
observed_state.distinctive: Code as first-class content. Severity color coding (red = fatal, orange = error). Breadcrumb timeline of user actions before the error. Inline file diffs.
